clusters.justification: These terms share robust co-activation within the circumscribed salience and broader affective network circuitries (e.g., insula, amygdala, anterior cingulate), collectively representing the overarching macroscopic functional domain of processing valence, arousal, and mood.

These concepts demonstrate dense co-activation across the ventral striatum, orbitofrontal cortex, and dopaminergic pathways, reflecting a unified macro-cognitive domain of cost-benefit computation, incentive processing, and goal-directed drive.

Relying heavily on shared co-activation throughout the core subsystems of the default mode network (e.g., medial prefrontal cortex, temporoparietal junction, precuneus), these variables represent a broad functional axis dedicated to internally generating abstract representations of oneself and others.
